skill.name: git-pr-workflows-pr-enhance
skill.description: Helps draft, review, and improve pull requests with clear descriptions, checklists, risk notes, and review guidance.
skill.summary: Improve pull request descriptions, review checklists, and reviewer context.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but semantic review found keyword and markdown false positives. The only confirmed risk factor is external command guidance for git workflows, which is expected for a pull request skill and does not show malicious intent.

low_findings.title: External Git Command Guidance
low_findings.description: The playbook includes Python subprocess examples for git diff and a bash workflow using git checkout, cherry-pick, and push. These commands are relevant to pull request analysis, but users should review branch names and repository state before execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are real external command patterns, but they are limited to expected git operations. I found no shell=true use, credential access, network exfiltration, or hidden execution path.

low_findings.title: Static Blocker Keyword Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported blocker hits are documentation keywords, headings, template text, or a Mermaid node named C2. No evidence found of weak cryptography, command-and-control behavior, system reconnaissance, or prompt injection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are visible documentation content such as PR templates, risk labels, and diagram text. They do not contain cryptographic code, reconnaissance commands, or malicious control logic.

dangerous_patterns.title: Python subprocess.run for Git Diff Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The playbook demonstrates subprocess.run for git diff commands. This is a legitimate PR analysis pattern, but generated agents should avoid executing commands without user context.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess calls are present and execute git commands when copied into tooling. The surrounding context is benign documentation for repository analysis.

content.value_statement: Pull requests often lack clear context, test notes, and risk details. This skill helps agents produce review-ready PR descriptions, checklists, and follow-up guidance.
actual_capabilities: Drafts structured pull request summaries with change context and review notes.
Builds context-aware review checklists for code, tests, configuration, and documentation.
Highlights large pull requests and suggests logical split strategies.
Frames risk assessments around size, complexity, dependencies, testing, and security.
Creates response templates for common reviewer conversations.
limitations: It depends on repository access for accurate diff and test information.
It does not replace project-specific review rules or maintainer judgment.
Example automation may need adaptation before use in CI or local scripts.
It cannot verify coverage changes without available coverage data.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Review-Ready PR
use_cases.description: Generate a concise summary, testing section, risk notes, and reviewer checklist before opening a pull request.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Review Quality
use_cases.description: Create consistent PR templates and review criteria across feature, bugfix, and refactor work.

use_cases.title: Improve Automation Notes
use_cases.description: Document automated findings, coverage changes, and split recommendations for large or complex changes.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Basic PR Description
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a single pull request.
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a clear pull request description for my current branch. Include summary, changes, tests, risks, and reviewer notes.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Review Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this PR context and create a checklist for code quality, tests, documentation, configuration, and security review.

prompt_templates.title: Assess PR Risk
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this pull request for size, complexity, dependency changes, test coverage, and release risk. Suggest mitigations.

prompt_templates.title: Plan PR Splits
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this branch and propose smaller pull requests. Group files by logical feature area and explain each split.

output_examples.input: A feature branch adds caching, updates API routing, and changes tests.
output_examples.output: A PR description with summary, changed areas, testing notes, architecture impact, risk level, and reviewer checklist.

output_examples.input: A large branch changes many files across frontend, backend, and documentation.
output_examples.output: A split plan that groups work into smaller PRs, explains review order, and calls out dependencies between changes.

output_examples.input: A reviewer asks why a specific implementation approach was chosen.
output_examples.output: A respectful response template that explains tradeoffs, alternatives considered, and the proposed next step.

best_practices: Provide the base branch, diff summary, test results, and any release constraints.
Ask for risk notes when changes affect authentication, data storage, dependencies, or configuration.
Review generated git commands before running them in a local repository.
anti_patterns: Using the skill without sharing enough change context for accurate PR documentation.
Treating generated checklists as a substitute for project review standards.
Running suggested command examples without confirming branch names and repository state.

faq.question: Does this skill create pull requests automatically?
faq.answer: No. It helps draft content, checklists, and guidance. Any repository action still needs explicit tool use.

faq.question: Can it analyze my current branch?
faq.answer: Yes, if the agent has repository access and permission to inspect git diffs or supplied change details.

faq.question: Does it support Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code compatible workflows.

faq.question: Will it detect all security issues in a PR?
faq.answer: No. It can add security review prompts, but it is not a full security scanner.

faq.question: Why is there a low security risk rating?
faq.answer: The playbook includes git command examples. They are expected, but should be reviewed before execution.

faq.question: What inputs produce the best results?
faq.answer: Provide the base branch, diff summary, changed files, test output, screenshots, and deployment context.
